commands.review_summary:
Adds four Codex/OpenAI auth-shaped strings to the shared failover auth matcher and regression coverage for two payloads.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +13. Total +17 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrow matcher gap: current main lacks explicit auth_unavailable and invalidated-token auth patterns, and the PR adds coverage for those payloads. I did not run a live dashboard/provider reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): classify Codex/OpenAI auth failures
target.body:
## Summary

Draft fix for #93272.

This teaches the shared failover matcher to treat Codex/OpenAI auth payloads as auth failures so configured model fallback can run instead of surfacing a generic `LLM request failed` response.

Covered auth shapes:
- `auth_unavailable`
- `authentication_error`
- `token invalidated`
- `token has been invalidated`

## Why

A local OpenClaw v2026.6.6 incident showed a failed primary model request did not advance to the next configured provider when the upstream error text was an auth-shaped Codex/OpenAI payload. The local hotfix was validated against the affected `heme-literature` dashboard session: primary `openai/gpt-5.5` failed, then fallback `bigmodel/glm-5.2` returned `OK`.

## Tests

- Added matcher regression coverage for Codex/OpenAI auth payloads.
- Local installed OpenClaw hotfix was smoke-tested with the affected agent/session.

## Draft note

This PR currently contains the narrow matcher fix only. The local hotfix also included runner-side propagation for zero-output assistant failures; that larger `run.ts` adaptation is prepared locally but not yet pushed here because my local git credentials are unavailable and the GitHub connector path is awkward for that large file update.
target.author: pandaaigc
